LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS PAR ANESTHESIOLOGY MEDICAL CORPORATION & MERTON GOODE, M.D.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THE ALLEGATIONS REGARDING A PREVIOUS INCIDENT (AMENDED COMPLAINT, P. 7:3-13 AND 9:9-19) ARE IRRELEVANT, FALSE OR IMPROPER AT THE PLEADING STAGE SUCH THAT STRIKING THEM IS WARRANTED. FURTHER, THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS OVERRULED IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER PREVIOUS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS PAR ANESTHESIOLOGY MEDICAL CORPORATION AND MERTON GOODE, M.D.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO JUNE 24, 2013. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS PAR ANESTHESIOLOGY MEDICAL CORPORATION & MERTON GOODE, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE COMPLAINT PLEADS FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CLAIM FOR HEIGHTENED REMEDIES UN THE ELDER ABUSE ACT (W&I CODE 15610, ET. SEQ.) MS. DAVIS HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE A CLAIM FOR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE BY ALLEGING RECKLESS CONDUCT. (CARTER V. PRIME HEALTHCARE PARADISE VALLEY LLC (2011) 198 CAL.APP.4TH 396, 406-07.)" 'RECKLESSNESS' REFERS TO A SUBJECTIVE STATE OF CULPABILITY GREATER THAN SIMPLE NEGLIGENCE, WHICH HAS BEEN DESCRIBED AS A 'DELIBERATE DISREGARD' OF THE 'HIGH DEGREE OF PROBABILITY' THAT AN INJURY WILL OCCUR. [CITATIONS.]" (DELANEY V. BAKER (1999) 20 CAL.4TH 23, 31-32.) MS. DAVIS ALLEGES THAT DR. GOODE ENGAGED IN RECKLESS CONDUCT BY FALSELY STATING TO A NURSE AND A CODE BLUE TEAM MEMBER THAT DR. GOODE HAD NOT GIVEN THE DECEDENT ANY MEDICATIONS PRIOR TO FINDING HIM UNRESPONSIVE. (FIRST. AM. COMPLAINT, 19, 26, 27.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LOREN E. SCHWARTZ OF EMISON HULLVVERSON LLP, TEL. NO. 434-2111 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. FRANCES BURNS OF LYNCH GILARDI & GRUMMER, TEL. NO. 397-2800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS PAR ANESTHESIOLOGY MEDICAL CORPORATION & MERTON GOODE, M.D.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. The complaint pleads facts sufficient to state a claim for heightened remedies un the Elder Abuse Act (W&I Code 15610, et. seq.) Ms. Davis has pled sufficient facts to allege a claim for dependent adult abuse by alleging reckless conduct. (Carter v. Prime Healthcare Paradise Valley LLC (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 396, 406-07.)" 'Recklessness' refers to a subjective state of culpability greater than simple negligence, which has been described as a 'deliberate disregard' of the 'high degree of probability' that an injury will occur. [Citations.]" (Delaney v. Baker (1999) 20 Cal.4th 23, 31-32.) Ms. Davis alleges that Dr. Goode engaged in reckless conduct by falsely stating to a nurse and a code blue team member that Dr. Goode had not given the decedent any medications prior to finding him unresponsive. (First. Am. Complaint, 19, 26, 27.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185
